violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH CFP at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Ground beef was placed hot in the refrigerator in a 2-inch perforated pan draining into another pan and covered with plastic. Plastic was removed from pan to allow food to properly cool. Proper cooling procedures reviewed with person in charge at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026
The slicer was observed in poor repair (chipped blade and worn-out spikes on grip). New Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026

Ham was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (cutting board) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Food was observed held at 45-49 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit near the toaster oven. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, all food had been placed in the prep unit about 3.5 hours prior to inspection. All food was relocated to another refrigeration unit. The ambient air temperature was observed at 41-44 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Prep unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Prep unit must be repaired/adjusted/replaced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Cheese was observed not marked with the date of preparation in the walk-in cooler. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Self-serve muffins, brownies, and cookies packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. The person in charge placed a sign informing customers to inquire about label information during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine on a cutting board) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
